primary_risks.description: Scalar/direct substitution may remain dominant despite procedural conversion pairs.
read_file-first targeting may collapse into local template recitation if diversity controls fail.
Exact-valid gains may over-concentrate around literal lexical shells instead of generalized commitment.
Over-regularized conversion prompts may narrow semantic flexibility and operational realism.
Positive procedural continuation patterns could bleed into no-call refusal contexts if unbounded.
Measured gains may reflect shell memorization rather than generalized procedural commitment.

mitigations: Deterministic contamination overlap blocking on heldout/tool_holdout
residual_risks: read_file may require multiple bounded iterations before exact-valid foothold stabilizes
Implicit uncertainty behavior may remain difficult to convert without over-regularization
Conversion pairs may still overrepresent a narrow subset of operational prompts

proceed_to_bounded_training_review_if: anchor dominance and top-shell concentration remain below thresholds
read_file exact-valid emergence is observed in bounded eval
further_dataset_revision_needed_if: hard blocks pass but scalar rebound proxies remain elevated
read_file concentration or shell concentration warnings trigger
abort_i10_direction_if: exact-valid gains appear lexicalized and non-generalizing
